text: This is the first installment of a multi-part video series for residents attempting to master skills on the robotic simulator. The goal of this series is to help residents improve their robotic skills by providing tips and tricks on how to ace the simulated exercises.
C-spikes one is an exercise that requires the placement of rings around cones in coordination with their colors. Keeping a larger view of the field and using both arms simultaneously is optimal.

The rings are usually presented in the manner and orientation seen here. You can see that by grabbing rings of the same color and prioritizing for the furthest corresponding cones helps to prevent confusion, improves your two arm coordination, and allows you to take care of the harder and most furthest cones first. Adjust your camera as needed, but continue to maintain a larger field of view. Also, utilizing your clutch is necessary in this exercise to maximize your arm reach and at the same time maintain good control.
You'll see the colors remain coordinated up until the last pair. And you'll see on the very last pair, the two foremost and easiest placement cones are there.

The scoring system for these exercises is based on several factors. Your timing and economy of motion provide a base score of a hundred. Any penalties are subtracted from this base score. You could see that we were efficient with the base score of one hundred and didn't commit any penalties to take away from that base score.
